aesthetic: a decaying grand theatre and the occult warren beneath it; faded Edwardian opulence gone to ruin — peeling gilt, moth-eaten velvet, thick dust and trailing cobwebs; gothic dread with deep chiaroscuro, heavy shadows and high contrast; dim, dingy interiors lit by weak grey daylight through cracks or feeble bare electric bulbs; a desaturated palette of dusty crimson, tarnished gold, brown and slate with deep near-black darks. Interiors are dry and dust-choked, NOT wet — dampness only where the prose says so (the cellars, tunnels and sewers below).

scenes.theatre-lobby: A grand theatre lobby, once-glorious and now decayed. The walls are hung with old faded posters of forgotten productions. A wide, sweeping grand staircase rises at the back of the room. The main exit is a doorway in the south wall; two small private doors are set in the northeast and northwest walls. Peeling gilt, dust, faded grandeur.
scenes.managers-office: A cluttered former manager's office: a heavy wooden desk strewn with loose papers, with more papers scattered across the floor. A closed metal wall-safe. Set into the floorboards is an open trapdoor, with the top of a wooden ladder protruding from it, descending into darkness below. A doorway in the southeast wall and another in the north wall. Dim and dusty.
scenes.basement: A dim cellar lit only by weak grey light seeping in from small street-level windows set high on one wall. Stacked wooden boxes and a couple of large barrels are the only contents. Set in the ceiling is an open trapdoor, with a wooden ladder descending from it down to the floor. An old electrical panel with a switch is mounted on one wall. Gloomy, dusty, with deep shadow in the corners.
scenes.outside-the-theatre: A run-down city street in a decayed slum neighbourhood at murky twilight. Litter is strewn across the cracked pavement and a slight mist hangs low. Boarded-up, deteriorating buildings line the street, which runs away east and west into the gloom. An empty parking space gapes at the kerb. The theatre's shabby frontage stands behind.
scenes.cramped-hallway: A narrow, cramped service hallway, its walls lined with stacked boxes of junk that pinch the passage tight. A doorway is set in the south wall and another in the west wall; the hallway continues north into dimness. Bare, dusty, dingy.
scenes.tight-stairway: A narrow, winding little service stairway. It climbs upward toward the west and descends toward the east. Cramped, with bare walls and dim light.
scenes.centre-of-long-corridor: A long corridor panelled in dark, aged wood, running north and south. A narrow little staircase descends to the east; a closed door is set in the west wall. Worn floorboards, dim and dusty.
scenes.south-end-of-long-corridor: The south end of a long, dark wood-panelled corridor; the corridor runs away north from here. A doorway in the south wall and another in the west wall. Worn boards, dim.
scenes.music-room: A cluttered former music room. Battered violin cases and tangled coils of instrument strings litter the dusty floor. An upright piano has been pushed up against one wall. A doorway leads out of the east wall. Dim, disused.
scenes.prop-room: A storage room for stage props: shelves crammed with assorted junk line the walls. A doorway is set in the north wall. A closed trapdoor is set in the ceiling overhead. Cluttered, dusty, dim.
scenes.southern-end-of-the-attic: The cramped southern end of a dusty attic. Rough wooden studs and crossbeams obstruct the low space; cobwebs and thick dust coat everything. The attic continues north. An open trapdoor in the floor leads down. Deep shadow, faint light.
scenes.attic-above-the-theatre: A dusty attic space over the theatre auditorium: bare crossbeams and a thinly plastered floor, cracks in the plaster glowing faintly with light rising from the auditorium far below. The attic continues south. To the north, mounted on a pair of sturdy crossbeams, stands a large winch with a hand crank; a thick cable runs from it down through a hole in the floor. Gloom and drifting dust.
scenes.attic-observatory: A bizarre attic observatory. Instead of a dome, the roof is a single, strangely patterned window. The exposed wooden wall-beams are set at unnatural, impossible angles, giving the unsettling impression of standing inside a large many-sided geometric solid. A large, oddly-shaped brass telescope stands in the centre of the room. An opening leads out to the south. Eerie, dim.
scenes.narrow-hallway: A narrow hallway where stacked boxes have collapsed into a large heap of junk. A pile of fallen carpet rolls lies tumbled across the passage, blocking the way north. The way back lies south. Musty, dim, choked with debris.
scenes.end-of-hallway: The end of a narrow, box-lined hallway. To the north is the theatre's back exit; the hallway continues south, and an office door is set in the west wall. Stacked boxes and dingy light.
scenes.alley-courtyard: A small enclosed courtyard at the centre of a city block, hemmed in on every side by tall, blank buildings that wall out any escape. Large old stage doors stand in the south wall, long disused and useless. A small back entrance is set in the southwest corner. A closed metal rubbish bin stands in one corner. Grimy, shadowed, open to a dim sky overhead.
scenes.staircase-landing: A grand staircase landing where the sweeping stairway splits to climb both east and west; the steps also descend back down to the south. A huge framed portrait dominates the north wall. Faded grandeur, dust, dim light.
scenes.secret-gallery: A cramped, dimly-lit hidden gallery, empty but for rows of old framed pictures crowding every wall. A small door leads out to the south. Close, shadowed, dusty.
scenes.eastern-landing: An upper landing that circles the balustraded rail above the theatre lobby, open over the lobby floor below. The landing continues south and west toward the stairs down. A set of double doors in the north wall lead toward the auditorium; a single, finely carved wooden door is set in the east wall. Faded gilt, dust, dim.
scenes.southern-landing: The southern end of the upper landing sweeping around the theatre's entrance hall, above the main doors and open over the lobby below. The landing continues to the northeast and northwest. Heavy closed curtains hang against the south wall. Faded opulence, dust, dim.
scenes.eastern-theatre-aisle: A long aisle running north and south through a vast, derelict theatre auditorium filled with rows upon rows of decaying seats. A parallel aisle lies to the west; the stage stands to the north; double doors lead out to the south. A great chandelier hangs over the auditorium. Cobwebbed, dim, cavernous.
scenes.eastern-stairway: A finely carpeted stairway, its once-rich runner now worn and dusty. It descends to a wooden door and climbs upward to the north. Dim, enclosed.
scenes.eastern-balcony: A private balcony box overlooking the cavernous theatre auditorium from the east, its faded plush seats set for the former elite. Far across the void to the west, the matching balcony boxes are just visible. The only way out leads down to the south. Below, a great chandelier hangs over the auditorium floor. Dim, vertiginous, dusty.
scenes.western-balcony: A long-abandoned balcony box on the west side of the auditorium, every surface buried under thick, undisturbed dust. In the rear wall a doorway has been bricked up; beside it, a small ragged hole has been knocked through the wooden wall, just large enough to crawl through, leading away to the northwest. Below, a great chandelier hangs over the auditorium. Deep gloom.
scenes.back-wall: The cramped, filthy interior of a hollow space within the wall dividing the theatre from the building beside it. Bare, dirt-caked timber and brick press close on all sides; almost no light reaches in. The hollow continues south, and a ragged hole opens to the southeast. Claustrophobic, near-dark.
scenes.wall-with-large-hole: A narrow hollow within the dividing wall, continuing north. A large, ragged hole has broken through to the west, opening into the neighbouring building. Dim, dirty, confined.
scenes.secret-library: A hidden library: rows upon rows of tall bookcases packed with hundreds of old books, the air thick with the smell of musty paper. Aisles lead away to the south; a small recess opens to the north. To the east a bookcase has collapsed, leaving a ragged hole broken through the wall. Dim, dusty, enclosed.
scenes.reading-recess: A small alcove that was once a reading nook: a single small reading table and a wooden chair are all that remain. The library continues to the south. Dim, quiet, dusty.
scenes.southern-end-of-the-library: The southern end of the hidden library, its bookcases draped and webbed with strange, glistening pale strands. A cold, damp breeze enters through a broken French window in the west wall; a library aisle leads north. The glistening strands all converge on a large nest of rotting, heaped-up books in the centre of the floor. Dim, damp, decaying.
scenes.street-balcony: An old stone balcony high above a side street in the abandoned quarter of the city. The view below is of boarded-up shop fronts and ruined, derelict buildings. Tall windows lead back inside to the east; the rough outer wall rises overhead to be climbed. Weathered stone, dim twilight.
scenes.theatre-roof: High on the steeply sloping rooftop of the theatre, beside a large brick chimney. All around, the rooftops of the abandoned city stretch away under a deepening dusk sky. The roof falls away dangerously on every side; a safe route leads west toward the balcony, and the chimney mouth opens downward. Windswept, precarious.
scenes.guest-star-room: A small but opulent guest suite, its furnishings of markedly finer quality than the rest of the ruined theatre: a fine carved four-poster bed and rich, faded fittings. A fireplace is set in the west wall. A door leads out to the east. Faded luxury, dust, dim.
scenes.sealed-off-office: A bare former office, all of its furniture removed but for a small fireplace in the west wall. A door that once led east has been completely boarded over. In the middle of the room stands an old-fashioned wooden coffin with brass fittings, resting on a mahogany stand. Stark, dim, dusty.
scenes.boiler-room: A cramped boiler room dominated by a huge old iron boiler, its pipes snaking out through and gripping the walls. A shaft rises from the boiler as the only way up; there is no other exit. A large, dirty coal bin stands against one wall, and a closed, grime-covered wooden chest sits nearby. Sooty, dim, oppressive.
scenes.north-end-of-long-corridor: The north end of a long, dark wood-panelled corridor, which runs away to the south. A doorway is set in the north wall and another in the west wall. Worn boards, dim, dusty.
scenes.costume-room: A crowded theatrical wardrobe room packed with racks upon racks of old costumes spanning every era, from Imperial Roman dress to World War I uniforms. Three costumed display mannequins stand among them — one dressed as a pirate, one as a cowboy, one as a gladiator. A doorway leads out to the east. Dusty, crowded, dim.
scenes.sick-bay: A small former sick bay. The walls are covered with old, faded medical instructional posters. A single iron infirmary bed stands against one wall, and a closed medical cabinet is mounted nearby. A doorway leads out to the south. Clinical, dingy, dim.
scenes.sealed-up-ticket-booth: A small room that has been sealed shut, its windows boarded over so that only thin blades of faint light leak through the cracks. In one corner lies a human skeleton in a ragged, collapsed heap. Cramped, grim, deep shadow.
scenes.stage: The wide stage of the derelict theatre, draped in cobwebs and thick dust, facing out over a vast auditorium of decaying seats. Aisles wind away through the seats to the southeast and southwest. The back-stage area lies to the north; the orchestra pit drops away below to the south. Cavernous, ruined, dim.
scenes.back-stage: A chaotic back-stage area, a maze of compartments divided by sagging ropes, wooden supports and hanging curtains, cluttered with rolls of old painted canvas scenery backdrops. The stage doors to the north have been boarded over and the stairs that once climbed up have been torn down; the only way out is back south to the stage. Cluttered, dusty, dim.
scenes.up-the-ropes: High above the stage, amid a tangle of dangling rigging ropes hanging down into the fly space. Across a narrow gap, a row of doors is set into the west wall — they once opened onto a landing long since gone. One door hangs close at hand. Vertiginous, dim, dusty.
scenes.dressing-room: A bare former dressing room; scuff marks on the dusty floor are all that remain of its vanished furniture. A single old metal locker, closed, stands against a wall. A door leads out to the east. Empty, dingy, dim.
scenes.orchestra-pit: A cramped, sunken orchestra pit below the lip of the stage, just large enough for a small ensemble. Steps lead back up to the stage, and an opening leads through to walk-in storage cupboards. Shadowed, bare, dim.
scenes.pit-cupboards: Dark, empty walk-in storage cupboards, bare but for shadowy gloom. The orchestra pit lies back to the west; a gap broken in the east wall opens to the east. Near-dark, close, dusty.
scenes.dark-place: A pitch-dark underground space; only the faint suggestion of rough passages leading away to the west and northwest can be made out. Near-total darkness, deep shadow.
scenes.under-the-stage: A cramped, low crawlspace beneath the stage; thin blades of light filter down through the cracks between the boards overhead. The ceiling is so low one must stoop. Small bits of debris that have fallen through the cracks litter the ground. A passage leads off into darkness to the southeast. Dusty, dim, confined.
scenes.cloakroom: A disused cloakroom; rows of empty coat hooks line the walls, now draped only with cobwebs. The way back to the lobby lies southwest. Bare, dusty, dim.
scenes.secret-stairway: A narrow secret stairway climbing up and descending down, feebly lit by a few bare electric bulbs set into the ceiling. Confined, shadowy, bare.
scenes.underground-passage: A dank underground passage lined with old, weeping brick walls. The passage climbs away to the west and runs east to a heavy, locked iron door. Damp, cold, dim.
scenes.above-the-pit: A huge, cavern-like chamber with a large rectangular pit sunk into its floor, like a drained indoor swimming pool. A passage leads west to an open iron door; down inside the pit, a passage leads off to the east. Vast, gloomy, rough stone.
scenes.inside-pit: The bottom of the rough-walled pit; its uneven sides make an easy climb back up and out. A gloomy tunnel leads away to the east. Rough stone, dim, shadowed.
scenes.tunnel-junction: A junction in a little network of rough-hewn underground tunnels. One tunnel runs west back toward the pit; others branch away to the northeast, southeast, and southwest. Cramped, dark, rough rock.
scenes.mine-shaft: A rough chamber resembling an old mine shaft, lit feebly by a dim electric light. Crude rock walls; tunnels lead off in all directions. Gloomy, raw stone.
scenes.deep-in-the-mines: A cavern deep in the mine workings, widened out, with signs of major past excavation. A large iron cage dominates the space. Rough rock walls, dim, deep shadow.
scenes.western-end-of-sewer: The western end of a large brick sewage trench that runs away east into the dark. A passage leads northwest, back beneath the theatre. This end terminates at a large iron sluice gate, below which a drain descends further into the muck. Wet, fetid, dim.
scenes.centre-of-sewer: The middle of a long brick drainage trench, which runs east and west. Above, to the north, a strange metal platform is fixed against the wall. Dank, wet, dim.
scenes.eastern-end-of-sewer: The eastern end of the drainage trench, where it dead-ends at a cluster of small, impassable pipe mouths. The trench runs back west; a tunnel leads off to the southeast. Wet, grimy brick, dim.
scenes.rat-nest: A small, filthy tunnel running northwest to southeast, its floor thick with rotting sewage and animal droppings. The tunnel ends at an unusual stone archway to the southeast, beyond which lies only darkness. Foul, dank, dim.
scenes.metal-platform: A raised metal platform fixed high on the wall, giving a clear view down over the entire brick sewer trench to the south. A long, rusty lever extends down from the ceiling within reach. Grimy ironwork, dank, dim.
scenes.witchs-lair: A small, circular chamber of red brick, domed like an igloo. In the centre stands an old iron cauldron filled with a dark, bubbling liquid. Shelves line the curved walls, crowded with little vials and strange knick-knacks — a witch's secret laboratory. The only way out is back to the northwest through a stone archway. Against one wall stands a large bronze statue of a four-armed woman; her elongated forehead bears four eye-sockets set in a diamond pattern, and each of her four hands grips a jewelled dagger. Claustrophobic, eerie, dim red glow.
